title: danthonia intermedia subsp. riabuschinskii
intro: Danthonia intermedia subsp. riabuschinskii, also known as Riabushinskii's danthonia or Danthonia riabuschinskii, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. Danthonia intermedia subsp. riabuschinskii is often prepared for medicinal use by making teas or infusions from its leaves or roots, and in some cases, it is used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
